title: Trade management
author: Head_Eagle5536
selftext:
Hiya legends!

Ive gotten majority of this strategy built already now I just need to figure out and code the trade management side of things.

For those who have been successful how did you find teaching the bot to know when to cut trades early or prevent bigger losses.

Im using a reversal based strategy on 1 minute candles and would typically manage trades based off CHOCH signals.

I’d appreciate any knowledge 🙏

comments.author: HellishGlassOperator
comments.body: on 1m reversals with CHOCH the cut logic that worked for me was time-based not just price-based. if the trade doesnt move in my direction within X candles after the signal, i exit flat regardless of stop distance. a lot of my bad losses before that were from trades that were technically still valid but just not moving, and then they'd eventually blow out. the CHOCH invalidation was always right in theory but too slow on 1m

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body:
Personally I've only made money on longer time frames. I've had modest success with a bot making one decision a day at 10AM. 

https://grok-trading-dashboard.vercel.app

comments.author: Cute-Let-4605
comments.body: It not AI decision logic in a real mgmt sense, but I use fixed profit targets and then a latch and trail approach to have a trailing stop after price moves past the “latch” - normally 2x risk.

replies.author: Head_Eagle5536
replies.body: Yup ive actually made two more models that have done pretty okay. One trading 1minute high volume candle sequences breaking past highs/lows of prior 5 hour candles. This usually results in a reversal or price retrace movement before it doing what ever tf it wants to do. With the 1 minute I have the SL anchored behind the extremes with buffer than 2 contracts. One going from 1R then next heading for max distance till price retraces back the other way.
